target.name: Atropos
target.founder: Colin Carroll, Vince Dutcavich, Kimon Roufas

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Atropos is a pre-seed stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector that has raised $6M to date. The valuation universe for comparable companies includes five private peers—Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and DroneForge—though no public comparables exist in this space.

Given the stage and data constraints, a single method was applicable to this analysis. Stage-Progression triangulation uses the $6M in total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of pre-seed-to-next-stage advancement in this sector, yielding an implied post-money valuation of $15M. A peer-median approach could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow peer set; expanding to all Drones & Autonomous comps would introduce substantial methodological noise given the heterogeneity of business models and development timelines.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $13M (low) to $17M (high), with a median of $15M. This range reflects reasonable variance around the stage-progression result but carries meaningful uncertainty given reliance on one method and the absence of traded or tightly comparable private transactions. Key unknowns include product maturity, capital efficiency trajectory, and whether near-term milestones or fundraising conditions warrant adjustment to standard stage multipliers.
